channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: [UN: July 17] EU’s Own Board of Peace, Diplomats Defend the ICC & Lots More
item.description: This Week @UN: The West’s allergy to “health equity”; women MIA from peacebuilding; India’s 9th Security Council bid. Plus: The EU’s Board of Peace; US bashes the ICC; UN criticizes Hamas; SecGen in China; US-Iran war reignited; affordable housing for you and me; Japan’s no to women.  Did someone forward this email to you? Subscribe ... Read more

item.title: The Oil Market Absorbed the War Shock, but Buffers Are Running Low
item.description: The largest disruption to the global oil market in decades should have sent prices soaring. But after spiking at the start of the war in the Middle East, crude prices soon settled in a range of $90 to $100 per barrel, much lower than many had feared. Why didn’t prices climb higher? The answer is […]

item.title: SERBIA: ‘We Are Becoming an Electoral Autocracy, a System Where the Government Cannot Lose’
item.description: CIVICUS discusses the prospects for elections in Serbia with Rasa Nedeljkov, Programme Director at the Center for Research, Transparency and Accountability, a civil society organisation that monitors electoral processes and the rule of law in Serbia. Following mass protests demanding restoration of the rule of law, Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić announced on 27 June […]

item.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
item.description: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.

item.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

item.title: Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike
item.description: More than 256,000 people in Djibouti – around 25 per cent of the population – are likely to face crisis or emergency level hunger over the coming months, a UN-backed global food monitoring network warned in a new report released Friday.

item.title: World News in Brief: Appeal for calm in Kashmir, women on the move, increased Ebola deaths in communities, 12th anniversary of MH17
item.description: The UN human rights chief, Volker Türk, has appealed for calm in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir amid unrest ahead of regional elections.

item.title: US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas
item.description: The UN Secretary-General remains deeply concerned by the continuing deadly military escalation between Iran and the United States, his spokesperson said on Friday as attacks destroyed key civilian and military infrastructure across the Gulf region.

item.title: As Gold Prices Soar, Communities Pay the Price in Poisoned Rivers, Vanishing Forests and Organized Crime
item.description: The sweltering heat inside a London conference hall did not deter Indigenous leader Jackeline Mendoza Díaz from condemning the sheer destruction of the Peruvian Amazon. Her voice occasionally trembled with emotion but delivered a strong message — painting a picture far removed from the glittering gold bars traded in the world’s financial capitals. Behind the […]

item.title: Renewed Hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz Threaten to Compound Global Supply Chain Costs
item.description: A continuation of hostilities within the Strait of Hormuz is once again threatening one of the world’s most critical supply chain arteries, posing another wave of disruption which could choke the global energy, shipping and commodity markets. With roughly a quarter of global seaborne oil trade transiting through the Strait, alongside significant flows of liquefied […]

item.title: Three years of War in Sudan: A Crisis the World Can’t Ignore
item.description: Three years into the war in Sudan, survivors and human rights defenders are struggling to respond to overwhelming needs amid widespread violence, displacement, and limited global attention. As horrific violations and abuses intensify and those documenting them become targets, calls for accountability and sustained international engagement grow more urgent. “The violations are severe: torture, rape, […]
